title: Rooftop Garden Trellis: Inducing a Nesting Rule that Generalizes to Taller Trellises
strategies: majority-label rule: a one-state DFA predicting the train-majority label everywhere -- reproduces the evaluator's baseline and shows zero ID->OOD generalization (~0.1)

suffix-window classifier: a DFA whose state remembers only the last k modules, labeled by local majority -- captures short-range patterns and beats the baseline, but cannot represent the global finite-state rule

RPNI-style grammar induction: build the prefix-tree acceptor then merge states in shortlex order (folding for determinism, rejecting label-conflicting merges) to recover the underlying automaton, which generalizes to much taller OOD trellises

evidence-driven / larger-window variants: EDSM-style scored merges, blue-fringe heuristics, or Occam-biased hypothesis selection to squeeze more generalization out of the under-determined sparse instances
